# @expressots/core

> ExpressoTS is a TypeScript-first Node.js framework for building server-side
> applications with dependency injection, built on Express 5. This package is
> the framework core: DI container, config, middleware, providers, testing.

Requires Node >= 20.19. Ships dual CJS/ESM. Uses legacy TypeScript decorators
(experimentalDecorators + emitDecoratorMetadata + reflect-metadata).

## Entry points

- `@expressots/core`: main API (bootstrap, AppContainer, decorators, providers)
- `@expressots/core/testing`: createTestApp, mockProvider, loadTest, matchers
- `@expressots/core/di`: Container, injectable, inject, scopes (Inversify-compatible)
- `@expressots/core/render`: EJS/Pug/Handlebars/React render adapters
- `@expressots/core/path-resolver`: manual path-alias resolution helpers

## Core usage (v4)

```ts
import { bootstrap } from "@expressots/core";
import { AppExpress } from "@expressots/adapter-express";

await bootstrap(App); // App extends AppExpress
```

- Providers: `@provide(ClassName)` class decorator; constructor injection via `@inject`.
- Modules: `CreateModule([Controller])`; app container wires modules.
- Config: `expressots.config.ts` typed by `ExpressoConfig` from `@expressots/shared`.
- Validation: optional peers zod, yup, class-validator; register adapters on
  `validationRegistry` (e.g. `validationRegistry.register(createZodValidator())`).
- Testing: `const app = await createTestApp(App)` then fluent `app.request()`.

## Do not use (removed or v3-only)

- `AppFactory` (internal since 4.0; use `bootstrap`)
- `BaseController`, `IMiddleware`, `ExpressoConfig` imported from core (v3 APIs)
- `InMemoryDataProvider` (removed; use `InMemoryDBProvider`)
- `LazyServiceIdentifer` typo alias (removed; use `LazyServiceIdentifier`)

## Docs

- Guides: https://doc.expresso-ts.com
- Source docs: per-module `.docs/` trees in the repository
- Changelog: CHANGELOG.md in this package
